intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: build a repo-owned, vendor-independent structured agent configuration substrate that planning, memory, and future behavior modules can attach to while prose startup surfaces remain compatibility adapters instead of primary authority.

required_continuation.activation trigger: reopen only if future repo-custom workflow needs require a new workflow engine, a second authority system, or stronger system-intent/adapter hooks than the current substrate can express compactly.

intent_interpretation.literal request: implement the next lane end-to-end, committing after each milestone
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: close the first roadmap lane through real shipped milestones and checked-in proof rather than leaving it as a loose parent-issue cluster.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: finish milestone 3 by formalizing repo-custom workflow obligations in workspace config, making adapter surfaces explicitly compatibility-shaped, making system intent a means-shaping and review-shaping layer in the substrate contract, closing issues `#269` through `#274`, and retiring the roadmap lane.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject the closeout if repo-custom workflow steps still live only in prose, if adapter surfaces still read like primary authority, or if system intent still acts only as high-level doctrine rather than shaping slice review and tradeoffs.

execution_bounds.required validation commands: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_source_payload_operational_install.py`; `uv run agentic-planning-bootstrap upgrade --target .`; `uv run agentic-memory-bootstrap upgrade --target .`; `uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json`
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before introducing a new top-level package, a new persistent runtime artifact family, or cross-module mutation machinery.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated recurring-friction work, machine-first planning-chain internals beyond the current lane boundary, or graceful-compliance work outside this lane.

stop_conditions.stop when: the next improvement would require turning workspace defaults/config into a scheduler-like workflow engine.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: closing the lane would require moving planning or memory domain ownership into workspace.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the work spreads into unrelated roadmap lanes or needs a new module family.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: the substrate still cannot answer the main startup, authority, workflow-extension, and review-shaping questions through compact workspace surfaces.

context_budget.live working set: issues `#269` through `#274`, workspace defaults/config/report outputs, the canonical substrate doc, root adapter surfaces, workflow-obligation config, and package/root contract checks.
context_budget.recoverable later: older architecture discussion and broader doctrine can be reloaded from summary plus the canonical workspace-config contract.
context_budget.externalize before shift: the workflow-obligation schema, the adapter-surface boundary, the system-intent means-shaping rule, and the fact that the lane is fully closed.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: the lane now closes because workspace owns structured substrate truth, compact queries, repo-custom workflow obligations, and prose adapters, while system intent stays a compact means-shaping layer.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: shift after proof passes and the issue/roadmap residue is retired.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: finish the lane by landing the remaining workflow-obligation, adapter-surface, and system-intent operationalization tranche, then retire the roadmap lane honestly.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: preserve selective adoption; keep prose as adapters; keep system intent directional rather than scheduler-like; keep planning and memory as consumers rather than workspace-owned workflow state.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: the minimal workflow-obligation schema, the smallest adapter wording that keeps prose non-authoritative, and the narrowest report/review hooks needed to make system intent and repo-custom obligations operational.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: lane closure would require a general workflow engine, a second config authority, or moving planning or memory domain ownership into workspace config.

execution_run.handoff source: roadmap lane plus live GitHub issues `#269` through `#274`
execution_run.what happened: milestone 1 established the canonical workspace-config contract and authority map; milestone 2 added selective-loading query classes and current-work report routing; milestone 3 added repo-custom `workflow_obligations` in workspace config and schema/report/defaults/config surfaces, updated the canonical contract docs so system intent now shapes implementation/review/follow-on means rather than acting only as doctrine, and made `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, and `llms.txt` explicitly adapter-shaped over structured config.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace config/schema/report/defaults/CLI/output code, canonical substrate docs in root and planning payload, root adapter surfaces, planning residue/evidence, and focused workspace/planning tests.
execution_run.validations run: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`; `make maintainer-surfaces`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_source_payload_operational_install.py`; `uv run agentic-planning-bootstrap upgrade --target .`; `uv run agentic-memory-bootstrap upgrade --target .`; `uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json`; live `gh issue view` plus `gh issue close` for `#269` through `#274`
execution_run.result for continuation: the lane no longer needs an open roadmap owner because the authority map, selective loading, workflow obligations, adapter surfaces, and system-intent shaping hooks now exist in the shipped substrate and the whole GitHub issue cluster is closed.
execution_run.next step: none; resume from the next roadmap lane only if a new lane is intentionally promoted.

finished_run_review.config compliance: respected the workspace-owned substrate boundary by keeping repo-custom obligations declarative and compact rather than building a workflow engine

proof_report.validation proof: focused workspace and planning tests passed, contract-tooling and planning-surface checks stayed clean, maintainer surfaces were refreshed successfully, source/payload/root-install boundaries stayed clean, both root upgrades succeeded, and the planning summary remained clean after retiring the lane.
proof_report.proof achieved now: the workspace substrate can now answer authority, selective-loading, workflow-obligation, and adapter-boundary questions directly, while system intent is queryable as a compact means-shaping/review-shaping layer.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: `workflow_obligations` now exist in config/schema/defaults/config/report surfaces; root adapter docs explicitly point back to structured config authority; the workspace-config contract now treats system intent as a compass that shapes means and review; and issues `#269` through `#274` are closed.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: build a repo-owned, vendor-independent, structured agent configuration system that planning, memory, and future behavior modules can plug into while reducing ambient prose authority
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: the lane now has a canonical workspace-owned substrate contract, compact query/report surfaces, declarative repo-custom workflow obligations, compatibility-shaped startup adapters, and a compact operational role for system intent.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the parent issue and all child issues for the lane now map to shipped substrate behavior and checked-in residue rather than open chat intent or prose-only reminders.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: commits `a640893` and `fd67e73`, this closeout archive, the updated workspace package contracts/tests, the refreshed adapter surfaces, and the closed GitHub issues `#269` through `#274`.
closure_check.reopen trigger: reopen only if future work shows that repo-custom workflow obligations, adapter compatibility, or system-intent shaping cannot stay compact without a new substrate shape.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: closed the structured agent configuration lane by landing workflow obligations, compact query/report wiring, adapter-surface authority boundaries, and system-intent means-shaping guidance in the workspace substrate.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`; `make maintainer-surfaces`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_source_payload_operational_install.py`; `uv run agentic-planning-bootstrap upgrade --target .`; `uv run agentic-memory-bootstrap upgrade --target .`; `uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json`
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none; the larger lane is closed
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: keep the rule that structured workspace config owns repo-custom workflow obligations and adapter authority, while prose startup surfaces remain compact compatibility routers.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (memory/docs/config): promoted the workflow-obligation and adapter-boundary rules into canonical docs, workspace config/schema/report/defaults/config surfaces, and the root startup adapters.
execution_summary.resume from: the next roadmap lane only if a new lane is intentionally promoted
